summary: Epic Morning Dew, strong second set, mixed first‑set
blurb: The second set soars with a monster‑sized Morning Dew, Victim Or The Crime and tight Terrapin Station. The first set is uneven, with lyric slips on Stagger Lee and Here Comes Sunshine. Overall the concert shines thanks to inspired Jerry and powerful percussion.
review:
On September 9 1993 at Richfield Coliseum the Dead delivered a night that’s remembered chiefly for its second‑set fireworks. After a patchy opening—Jerry stumbling through verses of “Here Comes Sunshine” and a thin bass line on “Stagger Lee”—the band regrouped, launching into a drum‑driven “Victim Or The Crime,” a blistering “Crazy Fingers,” and a soaring “Saint Of Circumstance.” That momentum carried straight into a tight, melodic “Terrapin Station” before the classic Drum → Space → “The Last Time” segue, culminating in one of the era’s most revered renditions of “Morning Dew.” While the first half shows a few miscues, the second‑set energy and emotional peaks make this show a standout for ’90s Dead fans.

For listeners seeking the definitive audio experience, the soundboard capture (gd1993-09-09.28781.sbeok.flac16) offers crisp, well‑balanced sound that captures the soaring dynamics of the second set despite occasional bass drop on “Stagger Lee.” Audience tapes like gd1993-09-09.schoeps‑evans.waddell.91949.flac16 provide an excellent rendition of “Here Comes Sunshine,” but the SBD remains the go‑to source for overall clarity and completeness.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings describe the show positively (monster‑sized Morning Dew, inspired playing, tight Terrapin Station) while one is mixed; overall sentiment is strongly positive across multiple sources, justifying a +1.4 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Only soundboard source; crisp, well‑balanced audio that captures the soaring second set and provides the most complete representation of the show.

key_highlights: Epic Morning Dew that ranks among the best ’90s performances.
Second‑set flow: Victim Or The Crime → Crazy Fingers → Saint Of Circumstance → Terrapin Station.
Strong percussion drive on Victim Or The Crime and overall powerful drumming.
Recording quality varies; the SBD offers clarity, while an audience tape excels on Here Comes Sunshine.

band_performance.Jerry: Early lyric stumbles on Here Comes Sunshine and Stagger Lee, but delivers powerful, inspired playing on Morning Dew and throughout the second set.
band_performance.Phil: Bass thin on Stagger Lee; otherwise provides solid foundation and adds “evil thunder sounds” during Dawn of Morning Dew.
band_performance.Billy: Drum‑driven energy shines on Victim Or The Crime, driving the momentum of the second set.
